name: Anna Kovács
note: Loves lush, jungle-style rooms and asks for a large ficus in every project. Prefers deep green foliage over anything with flowers.

name: Lena Fischer
note: Prefers architectural, sculptural plants that match her modernist furniture. Big fan of monstera and bird-of-paradise.

name: Jonas Weber
note: Beginner with a very small budget and a dark home office. Needs shade-tolerant plants like a peace lily or a cast-iron plant.

name: Sofia Rossi
note: High-end Mediterranean look for a sunny terrace. Wants olive trees and citrus, dislikes anything that sheds leaves indoors.

name: Diego Martín
note: Loves desert and arid themes. Asks specifically for cacti and succulents in terracotta pots, keeps watering to a minimum.

name: Lucas Dubois
note: Enjoys aromatic plants and a small kitchen herb corner. Wants basil, rosemary and a compact lemon tree if the light allows.

name: Katarzyna Nowak
note: Cozy, colourful style on a tight budget. Prefers flowering plants such as African violets and a bright orchid on the windowsill.

name: Petra Horáková
note: Wants an air-purifying focus for a family flat with kids. Interested in spider plant and areca palm, avoids anything toxic to pets.

name: Isabella Silva
note: Tropical, warm vibe for a small balcony. Loves calathea for the patterned leaves but knows they need humidity and care.

name: Sanne de Vries
note: Bright canal apartment with big windows and a designer look. Wants a statement monstera and a trailing pothos along the shelves.

name: Emma Andersson
note: Scandinavian minimalism, pale wood and neutral pots. Likes one bold fiddle-leaf fig and otherwise easy, low-light plants.

name: Matej Horvat
note: Small starter budget for a cozy studio. Prefers hardy, forgiving plants like a snake plant and a ZZ plant near the desk.

name: Elena Popescu
note: Warm, eclectic interior with lots of texture. Fond of ferns and a rubber plant, wants greenery that fills empty corners.

name: Niamh O'Brien
note: Classic style for a bright bathroom and hallway. Boston fern and maidenhair are favourites, loves anything that thrives in humidity.

name: Kristofer Nielsen
note: Premium, calm palette for a double-height loft. Dreams of a tall kentia palm and a mature ficus lyrata as the centrepiece.
